message.content:
Explore the directory /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tests/demos/ thoroughly (medium breadth).

For EACH demo script (*.sh) in that directory, report:
1. Filename
2. The one-line purpose (from its header comment) and which "mode" / "M-number" it demonstrates (e.g. M3 icount, etc.)
3. Which trigger mode it uses: csr, pc, icount, or slice
4. Which FIXTURE (ELF) it defaults to (look for `FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-...}"`)
5. What it asserts (exit codes, pc values, state-file byte offsets, register checks, output strings)
6. How the QEMU1 / vsim / QEMU2 legs are invoked (the key command-line flags passed to qemu-system-riscv64, the plugin, and the vsim binary)
7. Whether it exercises QEMU2 hand-back (3rd phase) or only the drains

Also report:
- Is there a demo runner or index? (e.g. how does `./hsim list demos` enumerate them, or a tests/demos/run_*.sh). Search for where the demo list is defined.
- A summary TABLE: demo filename | mode | fixture used | legs exercised.

I need to know exactly which fixtures each demo currently uses and how the demos differ from one another. Report file paths with line numbers for every claim. Read the actual files - do not guess.

- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- spec-human: Create a human-reviewable architecture spec using a 6-section template (Summary, Background, Architecture, Interface, Edge Cases, Alternatives) BEFORE implementing a non-trivial design. Triggered explicitly via /spec-human <topic>, or when the user says "write a human spec", "create a review-ready design doc", "spec this out for review", or "I need a spec for X before we build it". Produces an ASCII markdown document at ./specs/<slug>.md aimed at peer engineers and tech leads who need to validate core architecture and trade-offs in 5 minutes. Do NOT auto-trigger on general design questions or coding tasks - this is a deliberate review gate that only fires on explicit request. For LLM-targeted specs (future sibling skill), use /spec-llm instead.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T6: archive emit (M6) + checkpoint replay (M7).
3	#
4	# Step A (M6): orchestrator runs a single-spawn QEMU plan that drains
5	#   on the CSR-enter trigger and archives state.bin + mem + meta.json
6	#   under $WORKDIR/snap/0/.
7	# Step B (M7): orchestrator replay-checkpoint hydrates $WORKDIR/snap/0/
8	#   into a fresh /dev/shm mmap and resumes the chosen model (vsim by
9	#   default), proving the archive is portable across sessions.
10	#
11	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
12	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
13	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
14	#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
15	#   VSIM_ELF      C-level Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
16	#   WORKDIR       persistent dir for snap + replay state (default: mktemp -d)
17	#   RESUME_WITH   vsim | qemu (default: vsim)
18	
19	set -euo pipefail
20	
21	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
22	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
23	
24	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_c_v_regs}"
25	WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-archive-XXXXXX)}"
26	RESUME_WITH="${RESUME_WITH:-vsim}"
27	
28	MODE_LABEL="Archive emit (M6) + checkpoint replay (M7)"
29	echo "=========================================================="
30	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
31	echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs"
32	echo "Step A: orchestrator run plan -> snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}"
33	echo "Step B: orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with $RESUME_WITH"
34	echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
35	echo "=========================================================="
36	
37	hybrid_resolve_paths
38	if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
39	  echo "[demo M6+M7] SKIP: python3 not found"
40	  exit 77
41	fi
42	
43	mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
44	PLAN_YAML="$WORKDIR/plan_archive.yaml"
45	ARCHIVE_ROOT="$WORKDIR"
46	RUN_WORKDIR="$WORKDIR/run"
47	REPLAY_WORKDIR="$WORKDIR/replay"
48	RUN_LOG="$WORKDIR/run.log"
49	REPLAY_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay.log"
50	
51	# Single-step plan: QEMU drains on CSR-enter and archives.
52	cat >"$PLAN_YAML" <<EOF
53	session:
54	  mem_size: 134217728
55	  mmap_path: null
56	  elf: tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf
57	
58	steps:
59	  - model: qemu
60	    trigger: { kind: csr }
61	    on_exit: { archive_as: "snap/0" }
62	EOF
63	
64	echo "[demo M6+M7 1/4] orchestrator run: drain + archive"
65	mkdir -p "$RUN_WORKDIR"
66	set +e
67	PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
68	  python3 -m orchestrator.main run "$PLAN_YAML" \
69	    --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" \
70	    --workdir "$RUN_WORKDIR" \
71	    --archive-root "$ARCHIVE_ROOT" \
72	    >"$RUN_LOG" 2>&1
73	RUN_RC=$?
74	set -e
75	
76	if [ "$RUN_RC" -ne 200 ] && [ "$RUN_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
77	  echo "[demo M6+M7] FAIL: orchestrator run exited $RUN_RC"
78	  tail -30 "$RUN_LOG"
79	  exit 1
80	fi
81	
82	SNAP_DIR="$ARCHIVE_ROOT/snap/0"
83	for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
84	  if [ ! -s "$SNAP_DIR/$f" ]; then
85	    echo "[demo M6+M7] FAIL: missing or empty $SNAP_DIR/$f"
86	    ls -la "$SNAP_DIR" 2>&1 || true
87	    tail -30 "$RUN_LOG"
88	    exit 1
89	  fi
90	done
91	
92	echo "[demo M6+M7 2/4] validate meta.json schema"
93	META_OK=$(python3 - "$SNAP_DIR/meta.json" <<'PY'
94	import json, sys
95	meta = json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))
96	assert meta.get("schema") == 1, f"schema {meta.get('schema')!r}"
97	assert meta.get("abi_version") == 1, f"abi_version {meta.get('abi_version')!r}"
98	assert meta.get("abi_magic", "").lower() in ("0x42594841", "42594841"), \
99	    f"abi_magic {meta.get('abi_magic')!r}"
100	assert meta.get("mem_size") == 134217728, f"mem_size {meta.get('mem_size')!r}"
101	print("schema=1 abi_version=1 abi_magic=0x42594841 mem_size=128MB")
102	PY
103	)
104	echo "  $META_OK"
105	
106	echo "[demo M6+M7 3/4] orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with $RESUME_WITH"
107	mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WORKDIR"
108	set +e
109	PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
110	  python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP_DIR" \
111	    --resume-with "$RESUME_WITH" \
112	    --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" \
113	    --workdir "$REPLAY_WORKDIR" \
114	    >"$REPLAY_LOG" 2>&1
115	REPLAY_RC=$?
116	set -e
117	
118	if [ "$REPLAY_RC" -ne 0 ] && [ "$REPLAY_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
119	  echo "[demo M6+M7] FAIL: replay-checkpoint exited $REPLAY_RC"
120	  tail -30 "$REPLAY_LOG"
121	  exit 1
122	fi
123	
124	echo "[demo M6+M7 4/4] verify replay advanced PC past archive"
125	REPLAY_STATE=""
126	for cand in "$REPLAY_WORKDIR"/*state*.bin "$REPLAY_WORKDIR"/*.bin; do
127	  if [ -s "$cand" ]; then
128	    REPLAY_STATE="$cand"
129	    break
130	  fi
131	done
132	if [ -z "$REPLAY_STATE" ]; then
133	  echo "  no post-replay state file produced (replay completed at exit; OK)"
134	else
135	  ARCHIVE_PC=$(python3 -c "
136	import struct
137	print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', open('$SNAP_DIR/state.bin', 'rb').read()[16:24])[0]))")
138	  REPLAY_PC=$(python3 -c "
139	import struct
140	print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', open('$REPLAY_STATE', 'rb').read()[16:24])[0]))")
141	  echo "  archive pc=$ARCHIVE_PC, replay-drain pc=$REPLAY_PC"
142	  if [ "$ARCHIVE_PC" = "$REPLAY_PC" ]; then
143	    echo "[demo M6+M7] WARN: replay PC equals archive PC (no progress?)"
144	  fi
145	fi
146	
147	echo "[demo M6+M7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): snap/0 archived + replayed under $RESUME_WITH"
148

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T4: BBV-emit switching mode (M4) + SimPoint clustering integration.
3	#
4	# QEMU + plugin runs icount_spin.elf with bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M.
5	# Plugin writes one SimPoint .bb row per N retired insns; expected
6	# rows = M/N. Then tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint clusters the .bb file
7	# and produces simpts (slice -> cluster) + weights (cluster -> mass).
8	#
9	# Outputs are persisted under $WORKDIR (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)
10	# so T5/T7 can consume them. WORKDIR is printed on PASS.
11	#
12	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
13	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
14	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
15	#   VSIM_ELF      a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
16	#   SIMPOINT_BIN  the built simpoint clustering binary
17	#   WORKDIR       persistent dir for bbv/simpts/weights (default: mktemp -d)
18	#   SLICE         insns per BBV row (default: 1000)
19	#   ICOUNT        total insns before drain (default: 10000)
20	#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2; tiny BBVs need conservative K)
21	
22	set -euo pipefail
23	
24	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
25	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
26	
27	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-icount_spin}"
28	hybrid_resolve_paths
29	SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
30	WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-bbv-XXXXXX)}"
31	SLICE="${SLICE:-1000}"
32	ICOUNT="${ICOUNT:-10000}"
33	MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
34	
35	MODE_LABEL="BBV emit + SimPoint cluster (M4)"
36	echo "=========================================================="
37	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
38	echo "Workload: ${FIXTURE} (tight 2-insn loop, CSR-less)"
39	echo "Plugin: bbv_out=...,slice=$SLICE,icount=$ICOUNT"
40	echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
41	echo "=========================================================="
42	
43	if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
44	  echo "[demo M4] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"
45	  exit 77
46	fi
47	
48	mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
49	BBV_OUT="$WORKDIR/icount_spin.bb"
50	SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
51	WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
52	STATE_OUT="$WORKDIR/state_qemu.bin"
53	QEMU_LOG="$WORKDIR/qemu.log"
54	SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
55	
56	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
57	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-bbv-shared.XXXXXX)
58	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM"' EXIT
59	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
60	
61	EXPECTED_ROWS=$((ICOUNT / SLICE))
62	
63	echo "[demo M4 1/3] QEMU + plugin: emit BBV ($EXPECTED_ROWS rows expected)"
64	set +e
65	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
66	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
67	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
68	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
69	  -nographic \
70	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
71	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
72	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,icount=$ICOUNT,bbv_out=$BBV_OUT,slice=$SLICE" \
73	  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
74	RC=$?
75	set -e
76	
77	if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
78	  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: QEMU expected exit 200, got $RC"
79	  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"
80	  exit 1
81	fi
82	
83	ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV_OUT")
84	if [ "$ROWS" -ne "$EXPECTED_ROWS" ]; then
85	  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: expected $EXPECTED_ROWS BBV rows, got $ROWS"
86	  exit 1
87	fi
88	
89	echo "[demo M4 2/3] SimPoint cluster: maxK=$MAXK"
90	set +e
91	"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV_OUT" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
92	  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
93	  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
94	SP_RC=$?
95	set -e
96	
97	if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
98	  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: simpoint exit $SP_RC"
99	  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"
100	  exit 1
101	fi
102	
103	if [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ] || [ ! -s "$WEIGHTS" ]; then
104	  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: simpoint output empty (simpts=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS") weights=$(wc -c <"$WEIGHTS"))"
105	  exit 1
106	fi
107	
108	echo "[demo M4 3/3] Chosen phases:"
109	echo "  simpts (slice_idx cluster_id):"
110	awk '{printf "    %s\n", $0}' "$SIMPTS"
111	echo "  weights (mass cluster_id):"
112	awk '{printf "    %s\n", $0}' "$WEIGHTS"
113	
114	# Informational: weights should sum to ~1.0 (no hard assert; simpoint
115	# may emit weights in unnormalised form for some -maxK paths).
116	SUM=$(awk '{s+=$1} END{printf "%.4f", s}' "$WEIGHTS")
117	echo "  weight sum: $SUM"
118	
119	echo "[demo M4] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): rows=$ROWS, clusters=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS"), workdir=$WORKDIR"
120

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).
3	#
4	# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production
5	# SimPoint flow:
6	#
7	#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
8	#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...
9	#   M5 - per-phase    : QEMU+plugin slice=N,slice_at=K,outfile= -> drain
10	#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
11	#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
12	#
13	# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
14	# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
15	# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
16	#
17	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
18	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
19	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
20	#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
21	#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
22	#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
23	#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
24	#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)
25	#   PROFILE_ICOUNT plugin icount fallback for profile pass (default: 8000)
26	#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
27	#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)
28	
29	set -euo pipefail
30	
31	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
32	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
33	
34	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_c_v_regs}"
35	hybrid_resolve_paths
36	SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
37	WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
38	SLICE="${SLICE:-20}"
39	PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
40	PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
41	MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
42	# Defaults are tuned for rt_c_v_regs which reaches csrwi 0x7C0,0 after
43	# ~200-400 insns of QEMU1 startup. slice=20 gives ~15 BBV rows, enough
44	# for simpoint to find 2 clusters. Tune via env for other fixtures.
45	
46	MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"
47	echo "=========================================================="
48	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
49	echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
50	echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
51	echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
52	echo "=========================================================="
53	
54	if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
55	  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
56	fi
57	if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
58	  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
59	fi
60	
61	mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
62	BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
63	SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
64	WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
65	SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
66	SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
67	
68	# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
69	echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
70	SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
71	PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
72	PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
73	truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
74	trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT
75	
76	set +e
77	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
78	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM_PROFILE,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
79	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
80	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
81	  -nographic \
82	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
83	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
84	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$PROFILE_STATE,bbv_out=$BBV,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT" \
85	  >"$PROFILE_LOG" 2>&1
86	PRC=$?
87	set -e
88	rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"
89	
90	if [ "$PRC" -ne 200 ]; then
91	  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: profile QEMU expected exit 200, got $PRC"
92	  tail -20 "$PROFILE_LOG"; exit 1
93	fi
94	if [ ! -s "$BBV" ]; then
95	  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: BBV file empty"; exit 1
96	fi
97	BBV_ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV")
98	echo "  $BBV_ROWS BBV rows -> $BBV"
99	
100	# ---- M4-cluster: simpoint -----------------------------------------------
101	echo "[demo T7 2/4] cluster: simpoint -maxK $MAXK -dim 2"
102	set +e
103	"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
104	  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
105	  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
106	SP_RC=$?
107	set -e
108	if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ] || [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
109	  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: simpoint rc=$SP_RC; simpts size=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
110	  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"; exit 1
111	fi
112	NUM_PHASES=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS")
113	echo "  $NUM_PHASES cluster representatives:"
114	awk '{printf "    slice=%s cluster=%s\n", $1, $2}' "$SIMPTS"
115	
116	# ---- M5+M6: per-phase drain + archive -----------------------------------
117	echo "[demo T7 3/4] for each phase: drain at slice_at=K + archive"
118	PHASE_INDICES=$(awk '{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
119	declare -a ARCHIVES=()
120	for K in $PHASE_INDICES; do
121	  SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm "vsim-t7-phase${K}.XXXXXX")
122	  truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
123	  STATE="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_state.bin"
124	  Q_LOG="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_qemu.log"
125	
126	  set +e
127	  timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
128	    -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
129	    -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
130	    -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
131	    -nographic \
132	    -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
133	    -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
134	    -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$K,icount=$PHASE_ICOUNT" \
135	    >"$Q_LOG" 2>&1
136	  QRC=$?
137	  set -e
138	
139	  if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
140	    rm -f "$SHM"
141	    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: phase $K QEMU exit $QRC"; tail -20 "$Q_LOG"; exit 1
142	  fi
143	
144	  # M6: write snap/<K>/{state.bin, mem, meta.json} via the orchestrator's
145	  # write_archive helper (the canonical M6 entry point used by F9).
146	  SNAP="$WORKDIR/snap/$K"
147	  set +e
148	  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" python3 - <<PY
149	from pathlib import Path
150	from orchestrator.checkpoint import write_archive
151	write_archive(
152	    Path("$SNAP"),
153	    state_path=Path("$STATE"),
154	    mmap_path=Path("$SHM"),
155	    mmap_size=$SHM_SIZE,
156	    elf_path="tests/fixtures/$(basename "$VSIM_ELF")",
157	    source_step_index=0,
158	    cpu_variant="ax45mpv_premium",
159	    region_id=f"snap/$K",
160	    slice_index=$K,
161	)
162	PY
163	  AR_RC=$?
164	  set -e
165	  rm -f "$SHM"
166	  if [ "$AR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
167	    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: write_archive for phase $K (rc=$AR_RC)"; exit 1
168	  fi
169	  for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
170	    if [ ! -s "$SNAP/$f" ]; then
171	      echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $SNAP/$f missing/empty"; exit 1
172	    fi
173	  done
174	  ARCHIVES+=("$SNAP")
175	  echo "  phase $K archived -> $SNAP"
176	done
177	
178	# ---- M7: replay each archive --------------------------------------------
179	echo "[demo T7 4/4] for each archive: replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim"
180	REPLAY_OK=0
181	for SNAP in "${ARCHIVES[@]}"; do
182	  K=$(basename "$SNAP")
183	  REPLAY_WD="$WORKDIR/replay_$K"
184	  R_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay_${K}.log"
185	  mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WD"
186	  set +e
187	  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" \
188	    VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
189	    timeout 120 python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP" \
190	      --resume-with vsim --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" --workdir "$REPLAY_WD" \
191	      >"$R_LOG" 2>&1
192	  R_RC=$?
193	  set -e
194	  if [ "$R_RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$R_RC" -eq 200 ]; then
195	    echo "  phase $K: replay OK (rc=$R_RC)"
196	    REPLAY_OK=$((REPLAY_OK + 1))
197	  else
198	    echo "  phase $K: replay FAILED (rc=$R_RC)"
199	    tail -15 "$R_LOG"
200	  fi
201	done
202	
203	if [ "$REPLAY_OK" -ne "$NUM_PHASES" ]; then
204	  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $REPLAY_OK/$NUM_PHASES replays succeeded"
205	  exit 1
206	fi
207	
208	echo "[demo T7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): bbv_rows=$BBV_ROWS phases=$NUM_PHASES replays_ok=$REPLAY_OK workdir=$WORKDIR"
209

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T3: icount switching mode (M3) end-to-end.
3	#
4	# Both QEMU1 and vsim switch via retired-instruction count alone, with
5	# no CSR markers in the workload:
6	#
7	#   QEMU1 + plugin icount=N1    -> drain at insn N1 (M3).
8	#   vsim --hybrid-icount N2     -> drain at insn N2 (M3).
9	#
10	# Workload is icount_spin.elf (CSR-less tight 2-insn RVC loop at 0x100002
11	# and 0x100004). Neither side could use csrwi 0x7C0 -- icount IS the
12	# only available drain trigger. There is no natural semihosting exit
13	# (icount_spin spins forever), so this demo asserts the round-trip
14	# DRAINS correctly at both ends; M8 is exercised by T1 / T2 / T6.
15	#
16	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
17	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
18	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
19	#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
20	#   VSIM_ELF      CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
21	#   QEMU_ICOUNT   plugin icount drain target (default: 10000)
22	#   VSIM_ICOUNT   vsim icount drain target  (default: 5000)
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
27	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
28	
29	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-icount_spin}"
30	QEMU_ICOUNT="${QEMU_ICOUNT:-10000}"
31	VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-5000}"
32	
33	MODE_LABEL="Icount switching (M3)"
34	echo "=========================================================="
35	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
36	echo "Fixture: ${FIXTURE} (CSR-less tight loop)"
37	echo "QEMU1 trigger: plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"
38	echo "vsim  trigger: --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT  (CSR matcher disabled)"
39	echo "=========================================================="
40	
41	hybrid_resolve_paths
42	
43	SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
44	SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-rt.XXXXXX)
45	STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
46	STATE_V=$(mktemp)
47	QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
48	VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
49	trap 'rm -f "$SHM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
50	truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
51	
52	read_u64() { python3 -c "
53	import struct
54	with open('$1', 'rb') as f:
55	    f.seek($2); print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
56	"; }
57	
58	echo "[demo M3 1/2] QEMU1 + plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT -> drain"
59	set +e
60	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
61	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
62	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
63	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
64	  -nographic \
65	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
66	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
67	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT" \
68	  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
69	QRC=$?
70	set -e
71	
72	if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
73	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 expected exit 200, got $QRC"
74	  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"; exit 1
75	fi
76	if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
77	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 state empty"; exit 1
78	fi
79	
80	PC_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 16)
81	PC_Q_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_Q")
82	if [ "$PC_Q_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_Q_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
83	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 drained pc=$PC_Q_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
84	fi
85	echo "  QEMU1 drain: pc=$PC_Q_HEX"
86	
87	echo "[demo M3 2/2] vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT -> drain"
88	set +e
89	timeout 120 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
90	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
91	  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
92	  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
93	  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
94	VRC=$?
95	set -e
96	
97	if [ "$VRC" -ne 200 ]; then
98	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim expected exit 200, got $VRC"
99	  tail -20 "$VSIM_LOG"; exit 1
100	fi
101	if [ ! -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
102	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim state empty"; exit 1
103	fi
104	
105	PC_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 16)
106	PC_V_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_V")
107	if [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
108	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim drained pc=$PC_V_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
109	fi
110	echo "  vsim drain: pc=$PC_V_HEX"
111	
112	echo "[demo M3] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): both drains inside icount_spin ROI"
113

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T2: PC symbol-resolve switching mode (M2).
3	#
4	# Same round-trip as T1 but the orchestrator resolves
5	# _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the ELF symbol table and
6	# threads enter_pc=0x... to the QEMU plugin and --hybrid-exit-pc=0x...
7	# to vsim. The csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1} insns in the binary become inert
8	# (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on
9	# andes-ax45mp). Demonstrates that the same binary can be driven
10	# without any in-source trigger markers.
11	
12	set -euo pipefail
13	
14	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
15	
16	MODE_LABEL="PC symbol resolve (M2)"
17	echo "=========================================================="
18	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
19	echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs (C-level, V regs + V CSRs)"
20	echo "Trigger: nm-resolved _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"
21	echo "=========================================================="
22	
23	set +e
24	FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=pc EXPECT_RC=0 \
25	  bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/../e2e.sh"
26	RC=$?
27	set -e
28	
29	if [ "$RC" -eq 77 ]; then
30	  echo "[demo M2] SKIP (artifacts missing)"
31	  exit 77
32	fi
33	
34	if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
35	  echo "[demo M2] FAIL ($MODE_LABEL): underlying harness exit $RC"
36	  exit "$RC"
37	fi
38	
39	echo "[demo M2] PASS ($MODE_LABEL)"
40

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T5: slice-consume entry (M5) + icount exit (M3) on a CSR-less workload.
3	#
4	# Demonstrates that the orchestrator can use DIFFERENT trigger modes for
5	# the entry and exit of the same vsim window:
6	#
7	#   QEMU + plugin: slice=N,slice_at=K -> drains at end of slice K (M5).
8	#   vsim         : --hybrid-icount M  -> drains after M retired insns (M3).
9	#
10	# Workload is icount_spin.elf (CSR-less, predictable tight loop), so neither
11	# side can use the csrwi 0x7C0 marker. There is no natural QEMU2 exit
12	# because the workload spins forever; this demo asserts the round-trip
13	# DRAINS correctly at both ends. M8 is covered by T1 / T2 / T6.
14	#
15	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
16	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
17	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
18	#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
19	#   VSIM_ELF      CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
20	#   SLICE         insns per slice    (default: 1000)
21	#   SLICE_AT      0-based slice index for drain (default: 1)
22	#   ICOUNT_FALLBACK QEMU plugin safety upper bound (default: 10000)
23	#   VSIM_ICOUNT   insns vsim runs before exit drain (default: 5000)
24	#   SIMPTS        optional simpts file from T4; if set, slice_at picked
25	#                 from its first row (first column) instead of SLICE_AT env.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
30	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
31	
32	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-icount_spin}"
33	SLICE="${SLICE:-1000}"
34	SLICE_AT_ENV="${SLICE_AT:-1}"
35	ICOUNT_FALLBACK="${ICOUNT_FALLBACK:-10000}"
36	VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-5000}"
37	SIMPTS="${SIMPTS:-}"
38	
39	# If a SIMPTS file from T4 is provided, pick the first listed slice index.
40	# Otherwise fall back to SLICE_AT env (default 1).
41	if [ -n "$SIMPTS" ] && [ -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
42	  SLICE_AT=$(awk 'NR==1{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
43	  SLICE_AT_SRC="simpts(first row of $SIMPTS)"
44	else
45	  SLICE_AT="$SLICE_AT_ENV"
46	  SLICE_AT_SRC="env SLICE_AT"
47	fi
48	
49	MODE_LABEL="Slice-consume entry (M5) + icount exit (M3)"
50	echo "=========================================================="
51	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
52	echo "Workload: icount_spin (CSR-less tight loop)"
53	echo "Entry: QEMU plugin slice=$SLICE slice_at=$SLICE_AT (from $SLICE_AT_SRC)"
54	echo "Exit : vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT"
55	echo "=========================================================="
56	
57	hybrid_resolve_paths
58	
59	EXPECTED_QEMU_INSN=$(( (SLICE_AT + 1) * SLICE ))
60	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
61	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-slice-shared.XXXXXX)
62	STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
63	STATE_V=$(mktemp)
64	QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
65	VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
66	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
67	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
68	
69	echo "[demo M5 1/2] QEMU + plugin: drain at end of slice $SLICE_AT (insn $EXPECTED_QEMU_INSN)"
70	set +e
71	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
72	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
73	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
74	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
75	  -nographic \
76	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
77	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
78	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$SLICE_AT,icount=$ICOUNT_FALLBACK" \
79	  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
80	QRC=$?
81	set -e
82	
83	if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
84	  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU expected exit 200, got $QRC"
85	  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"
86	  exit 1
87	fi
88	if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
89	  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU state file empty"
90	  exit 1
91	fi
92	
93	# Verify drain happened at the slice boundary (plugin logs idx=K+1 target=K).
94	EXPECTED_IDX=$((SLICE_AT + 1))
95	if ! grep -qE "slice-consume drain \(idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT\)" "$QEMU_LOG"; then
96	  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU drain not at slice boundary idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT"
97	  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"
98	  exit 1
99	fi
100	
101	# Drained PC must be inside icount_spin loop body (0x100004 / 0x100008).
102	PC_Q=$(python3 -c "
103	import struct
104	with open('$STATE_Q', 'rb') as f:
105	    f.seek(16); print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
106	")
107	if [ "$PC_Q" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_Q" != "0x100004" ]; then
108	  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU drained pc=$PC_Q not in icount_spin loop"
109	  exit 1
110	fi
111	echo "  QEMU drain: idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT pc=$PC_Q"
112	
113	echo "[demo M5 2/2] vsim: resume + drain after $VSIM_ICOUNT retired insns"
114	set +e
115	timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
116	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
117	  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
118	  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
119	  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
120	VRC=$?
121	set -e
122	
123	if [ "$VRC" -ne 200 ]; then
124	  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: vsim expected exit 200, got $VRC"
125	  tail -20 "$VSIM_LOG"
126	  exit 1
127	fi
128	if [ ! -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
129	  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: vsim state file empty"
130	  exit 1
131	fi
132	
133	PC_V=$(python3 -c "
134	import struct
135	with open('$STATE_V', 'rb') as f:
136	    f.seek(16); print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
137	")
138	if [ "$PC_V" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_V" != "0x100004" ]; then
139	  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: vsim drained pc=$PC_V not in icount_spin loop"
140	  exit 1
141	fi
142	echo "  vsim drain: after $VSIM_ICOUNT insns pc=$PC_V"
143	
144	echo "[demo M5] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): both drains inside icount_spin ROI"
145

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""hsim - end-user entry point for the Andes hybrid simulator.
3	
4	Single-file driver. Wraps the developer-grade scripts (setup.sh,
5	tests/e2e.sh, scripts/run_e2e.sh) behind four subcommands:
6	
7	    ./hsim build [--what TARGET]
8	    ./hsim run <elf-path> --mode csr|pc|icount|slice [--verbose]
9	    ./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both]
10	    ./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>
11	    ./hsim doctor
12	    ./hsim demo <T1..T7|short-name> [-- KEY=VAL ...]
13	    ./hsim profile <elf> --slice N --icount M -o BBV

54	def parse_fixtures(makefile: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
55	    """Extract fixture names + family from tests/fixtures/Makefile.
56	
57	    Returns rows (name, family) in stable, family-grouped order. Family is
58	    the variable they came from (RT_FIXTURES, RT_C_FIXTURES, ...).
59	    """
60	    text = makefile.read_text()
61	    rows: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
62	    # Variable definitions look like:
63	    #   RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases \
64	    #                  rt_long_roi
65	    # We capture everything up to a non-continuation newline.
66	    var_pattern = re.compile(
67	        r"^(?P<var>[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*:?=\s*(?P<body>(?:[^\n]*\\\n)*[^\n]*)$",
68	        re.MULTILINE,
69	    )
70	    interesting = {
71	        "RT_FIXTURES":      "asm",
72	        "RT_C_FIXTURES":    "c",
73	        "RT_C_V_FIXTURES":  "c+rvv",
74	        "PROBE_V_FIXTURES": "probe",
75	    }
76	    seen: set[str] = set()
77	    for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
78	        var = m.group("var")
79	        if var not in interesting:
80	            continue
81	        family = interesting[var]
82	        body = m.group("body").replace("\\\n", " ")
83	        for name in body.split():
84	            if name and name not in seen:
85	                rows.append((name, family))
86	                seen.add(name)
87	    # Pick up the legacy hand-listed ELF targets.
88	    for name in ("handoff_roundtrip",):
89	        if name not in seen:
90	            rows.append((name, "asm"))
91	            seen.add(name)
92	    return rows

259	# F11: inline QEMU + vsim drain pair for --mode icount and --mode slice.
260	# e2e.sh only knows about csr/pc triggers; these CSR-less modes need a
261	# direct two-spawn path. Mirrors demo_icount_roundtrip.sh +
262	# demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh: both drain at exit code 200, vsim
263	# uses --hybrid-icount as the exit trigger.
264	_VSIM_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_S = 120

267	def _vsim_drain_argv(vsim: Path, elf: Path, state_in: Path,
268	                     state_out: Path, hybrid_icount: int) -> list[str]:
269	    """vsim drain argv with --hybrid-icount trigger.
270	
271	    No --shared-mem-* flags: the CSR-less workloads exercised by
272	    --mode icount and --mode slice (e.g. icount_spin.elf) re-derive
273	    their tight loop from the resumed state alone, matching
274	    demo_icount_roundtrip.sh and demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh.
275	    """
276	    return [
277	        str(vsim), str(elf),
278	        "--resume-from-qemu", str(state_in),
279	        "--handoff-out", str(state_out),
280	        "--hybrid-icount", str(hybrid_icount),
281	    ]

284	def _run_drain_pair(elf: Path, artifacts: dict[str, Path],
285	                    plugin_extras: tuple[str, ...],
286	                    vsim_icount: int, label: str,
287	                    mmap_size: int) -> int:
288	    """Two-step QEMU + vsim drain round-trip.
289	
290	    QEMU drains at its plugin trigger -> state file. vsim resumes from
291	    that state, drains after `vsim_icount` retired insns. Both spawns
292	    must exit `_QEMU_DRAIN_RC` (200). Used by --mode icount/slice.
293	    """
294	    try:
295	        shm_path = _load_hsim_shm().allocate(
296	            mmap_size, prefix=f"hsim-{label}-shm.",
297	        )
298	    except Exception as exc:
299	        print(f"hsim: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
300	        return 2
301	
302	    state_q_fd, state_q_name = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix=f"hsim-{label}-qstate.")
303	    os.close(state_q_fd)
304	    state_q = Path(state_q_name)
305	
306	    state_v_fd, state_v_name = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix=f"hsim-{label}-vstate.")
307	    os.close(state_v_fd)
308	    state_v = Path(state_v_name)
309	
310	    try:
311	        qemu_argv = _qemu_drain_argv(
312	            artifacts["qemu"], elf, artifacts["plugin"],
313	            shm_path, mmap_size, state_q,
314	            plugin_extras=plugin_extras,
315	        )
316	        print(f"[hsim {label} 1/2] QEMU drain ({','.join(plugin_extras)})",
317	              flush=True)
318	        proc = subprocess.run(
319	            qemu_argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT,
320	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
321	            timeout=_PROFILE_TIMEOUT_S,
322	        )
323	        if proc.returncode != _QEMU_DRAIN_RC:
324	            tail = proc.stdout.decode("utf-8", "replace").splitlines()[-20:]
325	            print(f"hsim: QEMU {label} expected exit {_QEMU_DRAIN_RC}, "
326	                  f"got {proc.returncode}", file=sys.stderr)
327	            for line in tail:
328	                print(line, file=sys.stderr)
329	            return 1
330	
331	        print(f"[hsim {label} 2/2] vsim --hybrid-icount {vsim_icount} drain",
332	              flush=True)
333	        vsim_argv = _vsim_drain_argv(
334	            artifacts["vsim"], elf, state_q, state_v, vsim_icount,
335	        )
336	        proc = subprocess.run(
337	            vsim_argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT,
338	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
339	            timeout=_VSIM_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_S,
340	        )
341	        if proc.returncode != _QEMU_DRAIN_RC:
342	            tail = proc.stdout.decode("utf-8", "replace").splitlines()[-20:]
343	            print(f"hsim: vsim {label} expected exit {_QEMU_DRAIN_RC}, "
344	                  f"got {proc.returncode}", file=sys.stderr)
345	            for line in tail:
346	                print(line, file=sys.stderr)
347	            return 1
348	
349	        print(f"PASS: --mode {label} ({elf.name})")
350	        return 0
351	    finally:
352	        for p in (shm_path, state_q, state_v):
353	            try:
354	                p.unlink()
355	            except FileNotFoundError:
356	                pass

432	def _read_hybrid_toolchain() -> str | None:
433	    """Locate HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN: env first, then config.env fallback."""
434	    tc = os.environ.get("HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN")
435	    if tc:
436	        return tc
437	    cfg = REPO_ROOT / "config.env"
438	    if not cfg.is_file():
439	        return None
440	    pattern = re.compile(r"^\s*(?:export\s+)?HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN\s*=\s*(.+?)\s*$")
441	    for line in cfg.read_text().splitlines():
442	        m = pattern.match(line)
443	        if m:
444	            return m.group(1).strip().strip('"').strip("'")
445	    return None

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# End-to-end roundtrip harness: QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
3	#
4	# Memory between QEMU and vsim is shared via an /dev/shm-backed mmap
5	# (128 MB). Works for both asm fixtures (handoff_roundtrip, rt_*) and
6	# C-level fixtures (rt_c_*). C fixtures require shared mmap so the
7	# stack/heap survives handoffs; asm fixtures don't need it but run
8	# fine with it.
9	#
10	# Env:
11	#   FIXTURE         REQUIRED. .elf name under tests/fixtures/ (no .elf suffix).
12	#   EXPECT_RC       Final exit code to assert (default 0).
13	#   TRIGGER_MODE    csr | pc (default csr).
14	#   TIMEOUT         vsim leg timeout in seconds (default 60).
15	#   CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1   assert mcycle/minstret strictly increased
16	#                              between QEMU 1's drain and vsim's drain.
17	#   VSIM_BIN, VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM  override built paths.
18	
19	set -euo pipefail
20	
21	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
22	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/paths.sh"
23	
24	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:?FIXTURE env var is required (e.g. FIXTURE=rt_c_hello)}"
25	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
26	TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
27	TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"
28	
29	hybrid_resolve_paths
30	
31	NM="${NM:-${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:+$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}}"
32	NM="${NM:-riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"
33	
34	case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
35	  csr) ;;
36	  pc)
37	    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
38	      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
39	      exit 77
40	    fi
41	    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
42	    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
43	    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT"  | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
44	    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
45	      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
46	      exit 1
47	    fi
48	    ;;
49	  *)
50	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
51	    exit 1
52	    ;;
53	esac
54	
55	# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim uses the same size.
56	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
57	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-e2e-shared.XXXXXX)
58	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
59	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
60	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
61	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
62	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
63	
64	PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
65	VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
66	if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
67	  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
68	  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
69	fi
70	
71	echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state"
72	set +e
73	"$VSIM_QEMU" \
74	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
75	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
76	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
77	  -nographic \
78	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
79	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
80	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
81	QEMU1_RC=$?
82	set -e
83	
84	if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
85	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
86	  exit 1
87	fi
88	
89	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
90	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
91	  exit 1
92	fi
93	
94	echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
95	set +e
96	timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
97	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
98	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
99	  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
100	  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
